manuscript_profile.title: Pride and Prejudice
manuscript_profile.genre: Adult Literary Social Comedy / Courtship Novel
manuscript_profile.target_audience: Adult readers of literary social comedy, courtship novel, Regency fiction, and moral/satirical novels of manners

txt: Adult Literary Social Comedy / Courtship Novel
Literary Social Comedy / Courtship Novel / Novel of Manners
A brilliantly durable social premise: marriage, money, rank, and misjudgment force moral education inside a comic courtship structure.
It should not be evaluated as a generic enemies-to-lovers romance; the romance is inseparable from social intelligence, money, family exposure, and self-correction.
Free indirect discourse and ironic narration
Author retains ownership of manuscript content

optional_public_strings: Social perception can be plot, and dialogue, manners, letters, visits, houses, and reputation can carry full narrative causality.
Late remediation of the Lydia/Wickham crisis is partly offstage.
source_only_strings: Pride and Prejudice teaches the evaluator that social perception can be plot.
Do not treat marriage as decorative reward; marriage is the period's economic, social, and reputational pressure system.
